verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And as they spake unto the people, there came unto them the priests, and the captain of the temple.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Ere yet they had time to take breath after their first trials, straightway they enter into others.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And observe how the events are disposed.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: First, they were all mocked together; this was no small trial: secondly, they enter into dangers.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And these two things do not take place in immediate succession; but when first the Apostles have won admiration by their two discourses, and after that have performed a notable miracle, thereupon it is that, after they are waxen bold, through God’s disposal, they enter the lists.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But I wish you to consider, how those same persons, who in the case of Christ must need look out for one to deliver Him up to them, now with their own hands arrest the Apostles, having become more audacious and more impudent since the Crucifixion.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: In truth, sin, while it is yet struggling to the birth, is attended with some sense of shame; but when once fully born, it makes those more shameless who practise it.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And the captain of the temple,” it is said.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The object again was to attach a public criminality to what was doing, and not to prosecute it as the act of private individuals: such in fact was constantly their plan of proceeding.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Being grieved that they taught the people.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Not merely because they taught, but because they declared, not alone that Christ Himself was risen from the dead, but moreover, that we through Him do rise again.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Because they taught the people, and preached through Jesus the resurrection of the dead.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: So mighty was His Resurrection, that to others also He is the cause of a resurrection.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And they laid hands on them, and put them in hold unto the next day; for it was now eventide. (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: What impudence!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: They feared not the multitude; for this also the captain of the temple was with them: they had their hands still reeking with the blood of the former victim.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “For it was now eventide,” it is said.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: It was with the wish to abate their spirit that those men did this, and guarded them; but the delay only served to make the Apostles more intrepid.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And consider who these are who are arrested.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: They are the chiefs of the Apostles, who are now become a pattern to the rest, that they should no longer crave each other’s support, nor want to be together.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Howbeit, many having heard the word, believed; and the number of the men was about five thousand.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: How was this?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Did they see them in honor?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Did they not behold them put in bonds?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: How then did they believe?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Do you see the evident efficacy?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And yet even those that believed already might well have become weaker.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But no, it is no longer so: for Peter’s sermon had laid the seed deep into them, and had taken a hold upon their understandings.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Therefore were [their enemies] incensed, that they did not fear them, that they made no account of their present troubles.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For, say they, if He that was crucified effects such great things, and makes the lame to walk, we fear not these men either.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: This again is of God’s ordering.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For those who now believe were more numerous than the former.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Therefore it was that in their presence they bound the Apostles, to make them also more fearful.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But the reverse took place.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And they examine them not before the people, but privately, that the hearers may not profit by their boldness.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And it came to pass on the morrow, that their rulers, and elders, and scribes, and Annas the High Priest, and Caiaphas, and John, and Alexander, and as many as were of the kindred of the High Priest, were gathered together at Jerusalem.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For now along with the other evils (of the times), the Law was no longer observed.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And again they set off the business with the form of a tribunal, to constitute them guilty by their iniquitous sentence.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And when they had set them in the midst, they asked, By what power, or by what name, have ye done this?” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And yet they knew it well; for it was because they were “grieved that they preached through Jesus the resurrection” that they arrested them.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Then for what purpose do they question them?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: They expected the numbers present would make them recant, and thought by this means to have put all right again.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Observe then what they say: “And by what name have ye done this?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Then Peter, filled with the Holy Ghost, said unto them.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And now, I pray you, call to mind Christ’s saying; “When they deliver you up unto the synagogues, take ye no thought how or what thing ye shall speak; for it is the Spirit of your Father which speaketh in you.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: So that it was a mighty Power they enjoyed.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: What then says Peter?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Ye rulers of the people, and elders of Israel.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Mark the Christian wisdom of the man; how full of confidence it is: he utters not a word of insult, but says with respect, “Ye rulers of the people, and elders of Israel, if we be this day called to account of the good deed done to the impotent man.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: He takes them in hand right valiantly; by the opening of his speech he exposes them, and reminds them of the former things: that it is for a work of beneficence they are calling them to account.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: As if he had said, “In all fairness we ought to have been crowned for this deed, and proclaimed benefactors; but since “we are even put upon our trial for a good deed done to an impotent man,” not a rich man, not powerful, not noble—and yet who would feel envy in a case like this?”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: It is a most forcible (ἀπαγγελια, al. ἐπαγγελία) way of putting the case; and he shows that they are piercing their own selves:—“By what means this man is made whole: be it known unto you all, and to all the people Israel; that by the Name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth:”—this is what would vex them most.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For this was that which Christ had told the disciples, “What ye hear in the ear that preach ye upon the housetops.—That in the name of Jesus Christ,” he says, “of Nazareth, Whom ye crucified, Whom God raised from the dead, even by Him doth this man stand here before you whole.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Think not, he says that we conceal the country, or the nature of the death.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Whom ye crucified, Whom God raised from the dead, even by Him doth this man stand before you whole.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Again the death, again the resurrection.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “This is the stone,” he says, “which was set at nought of you builders, which is become the head of the corner.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: He reminds them also of a saying which was enough to frighten them.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For it had been said, “Whosoever shall fall on this stone shall be broken; but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: 44.)—Neither is there salvation in any other, (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: What wounds, think you, must these words inflict on them!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “For there is none other name,” he continues, “under heaven given among men, whereby we must be saved.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Here he utters also lofty words.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For when the object is, not to carry some point successfully, but only to show boldness he does not spare; for he was not afraid of striking too deep.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Nor does he say simply, “By another;” but, “Neither is there salvation in any other:” that is, He is able to save us.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: In this way he subdued their threatening.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Now when they saw the boldness of Peter and John, and perceived that they were unlearned and ignorant men, they marvelled and they took knowledge of them, that they had been with Jesus.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The two unlearned men beat down with their rhetoric them and the chief priests.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For it was not they that spake, but the grace of the Spirit.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And beholding the man which was healed standing with them, they could say nothing against it.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Great was the boldness of the man; that even in the judgment-hall he has not left them.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For had they said that the fact was not so, there was he to refute them.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “But when they had commanded them to go aside out of the council, they conferred among themselves, saying, What are we to do to these men?” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: See the difficulty they are in, and how the fear of men again does everything.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: As in the case of Christ, they were not able (as the saying is) to undo what is done, nor to cast it into the shade, but for all their hindering, the Faith did but gain ground the more; so was it now.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “What shall we do?”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: O the folly! to suppose that those who had tasted of the conflict, would now take fright at it: to expect, impotent as their efforts had proved in the beginning, to effect something new, after such a specimen of oratory as had been exhibited!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The more they wished to hinder, the more the business grew upon their hands.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But what say they?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “For that indeed a notable miracle hath been done by them is manifest to all them that dwell in Jerusalem; and we cannot deny it.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But that it spread no further among the people, let us straightly threaten them, that they speak henceforth to no man in this name.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And they called them, and commanded them not to speak at all, nor teach, in the name of Jesus.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: See what effrontery is shown by these, and what greatness of mind by the Apostles.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “But Peter and John answered and said unto them, Whether it be right in the sight of God to hearken unto you more than unto God, judge ye.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For we cannot but speak the things which we have seen and heard.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: So when they had further threatened them, they let them go, finding nothing how they might punish them, because of the people.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The miracles shut their mouths: they would not so much as let them finish their speech, but cut them short in the middle, most insolently.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “For all men glorified God for that which was done.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For the man was above forty years old, on whom this miracle of healing was showed.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But let us look over what has been said from the beginning.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And as they spake unto the people, etc. Being grieved that they taught the people, and preached through Jesus the resurrection of the dead.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: (Recapitulation, v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: So then at first they did all for the sake of man’s opinion (or glory): but now another motive was added: that they should not be thought guilty of murder, as they said subsequently, “Do ye wish to bring this man’s blood on us?” (ch. v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: O the folly!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Persuaded that He was risen, and having received this proof of it, they expected that He Whom death could not hold, could be cast into the shade by their machinations!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: What can match the folly of this!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Such is the nature of wickedness: it has no eyes for anything, but on all occasions it is thrown into perturbation.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Finding themselves overborne, they felt like persons who have been outwitted: as is the case with people who have been forestalled and made a sport of in some matter.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And yet they everywhere affirmed that it was God that raised Him: but it was “in the Name of Jesus” that they spake; showing that Jesus was risen.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Through Jesus, the resurrection of the dead”: for they themselves also held a resurrection: a cold and puerile doctrine, indeed, but still they held it.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Why this alone, was it not sufficient to induce them to do nothing to them—I mean, that the disciples with such boldness bore themselves in the way they did?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Say, wherefore, O Jew, dost thou disbelieve?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Thou oughtest to have attended to the sign done, and to the words, not to the evil disposition of the many.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “By their teaching the people.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For already they were in ill repute with them by reason of what they had done to Christ; so that they were rather increasing their own obloquy.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And they laid hands on them, and put them in hold until the morrow; for it was now eventide.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: In the case of Christ, however, they did not so; but having taken Him at midnight, they immediately led him away, and made no delay, being exceedingly in fear of the multitude: whereas in the case of the Apostles here, they were bold.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And they no more take them to Pilate, being ashamed and blushing at the thought of the former affair, lest they should also be taken to task for that.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And it came to pass on the morrow, that their rulers, and elders, and scribes were gathered together at Jerusalem.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Again in Jerusalem: and there it is that men’s blood is poured out; no reverence for their city either; “And Annas, and Caiaphas,” etc. (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And Annas,” it says, “and Caiaphas.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: His maid-servant it was that questioned Peter, and he could not bear it: in his house it was that Peter denied, when Another was in bonds there: but now, when he has come into the midst of them all, see how he speaks!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “By what name have ye done this?”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Why dost thou not speak it, what it is, but keepest that out of sight?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “By what name have ye done this?” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And yet he affirmed, It was not we that did it.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Ye rulers of the people,” etc. (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Observe his wisdom: he does not say outright, “In the Name of Jesus we did it,” but how?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “In His Name this man”—He does not say, “was made whole by us;” but—“doth stand here before you whole.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And again, “If we be examined concerning the good deed done to the impotent man.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: He hits them hard, that they are always making a crime of such acts, finding fault with works of beneficence done to men: and he reminds them of their former doings, that they run to do murder, and not only so, but make a crime of doing good deeds.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Do you observe too (in point of rhetoric) with what dignity they express themselves?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Even in the use of words they were becoming expert by practice, and henceforth they were not to be beaten down.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Be it known unto you all,” etc. (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Whereby he shows them that they rather do, in spite of themselves, preach Christ; themselves extol the doctrine, by their examining and questioning.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: O exceeding boldness—“Whom ye crucified!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Whom God raised up”—this is bolder still!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Think not that we hide what there is to be ashamed of.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: He says this all but tauntingly: and not merely says it, but dwells upon the matter.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “This,” says he, “is the Stone which was set at naught by you builders;” and then he goes on to teach them, saying in addition, “Which is made the head of the corner” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: 11.); that is to say, that the Stone is indeed approved!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Great was the boldness they now had, in consequence of the miracle.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And when there was need to teach, observe how they speak and allege many prophecies; but when the point was to use boldness of speech, then they only speak peremptorily.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Thus “Neither,” says he, “is there any other name under heaven given among men whereby we must be saved.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: It is manifest to all, he says, because not to us alone was that Name given; he cites even themselves as witnesses.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For, since they asked, “In what name did ye it?”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “In Christ’s,” says he: “there is none other name.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: How is it that ye ask?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: On all hands this is palpable.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “For there exists not another name under heaven, whereby we must be saved.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: This is the language of a soul which has renounced (κατεγνωκυίας) this present life.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: His exceeding out-spokenness proves here, that when he speaks in lowly terms of Christ, he does it not of fear, but of wise forbearance (συγκαταβαίνων): but now that it was the fitting time, he speaks not in lowly terms: by this very thing intending to strike dismay into them.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Behold another miracle not less than the former.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And beholding the boldness of Peter and John,” etc. “And they took knowledge of them that they had been with Jesus.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Not without a meaning has the Evangelist set down this passage; but in saying, “they recognized them that they had been with Jesus,” he means, in His Passion: for only these were [with Him] at that time, and then indeed they had seen them humble, dejected: and this it was that most surprised them: the greatness of the change.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For in fact Annas and Caiaphas with their company were there, and these then also had stood by Him, and their boldness now amazed them.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And beholding the boldness.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For not only their words; their very bearing showed it; that they should stand there so intrepidly to be tried in a cause like this, and with uttermost peril impending over them!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Not only by their words, but by their gesture also, and their look and voice, and, in short, by everything about them, they manifested the boldness with which they confronted the people.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: From the things they uttered, they marvelled, perhaps: “that they were unlearned and common men:” for one may be unlearned, yet not a common or private man, and a common man, yet not unlearned.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Having perceived,” it says.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: From what they said?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Peter does not draw out long speeches, but then by his very manner and method (τἥς ἀπαγγελίας καὶ τἥς συνθήκης) he declares his confidence.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And they recognized them that they had been with Jesus.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Which circumstance made them believe that it was from Him they had learned these things, and that they did all in the character of His disciples.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But not less than the voice of these, the miracle uttered a voice of its own: and that sign itself stopped their mouths. [“And beholding the man,” etc.] So that they would have been peremptory (ἐπέσκηψαν) with them, if the man had not been with them.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “We cannot deny it.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: So that they would have denied it, if the thing had not been so: if the testimony had not been that of the people in general.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “But that it spread no further among the people.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And yet it was palpable to all men!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But such is the nature of wickedness: everywhere it is shamed.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Let us straitly threaten them.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: What sayest thou?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And expect ye to stop the preaching?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And yet all beginnings are hard and trying.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Ye slew the Master, and did not stop it: and now, if ye threaten, do ye expect to turn us back?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The imprisonment did not prevail with us to speak submissively, and shall ye prevail?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And they called them, and commanded them,” etc. (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: It had been much better for them to let them go.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And Peter and John answered and said unto them, Whether it be right in the sight of God to hearken unto you more than unto God, judge ye.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: When the terror was abated (for that command was tantamount to their being dismissed), then also the Apostles speak more mildly: so far were they from mere bravery: “Whether it be right,” says he: and “We cannot [but speak].

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Whether it be right in the sight of God to obey you rather than God.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Here [by “God”] they mean Christ, for he it was that commanded them.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And once more they confirm the fact of His Resurrection.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “For we cannot but speak the things we have seen and heard:” so that we are witnesses who have a right to be believed.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “So when they had further threatened them.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Again they threatened in vain.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “They let them go, finding nothing how they might punish them, because of the people: for all men glorified God for that which was done.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: So then the people glorified God, but these endeavored to destroy them: such fighters against God were they!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Whereby they made them more conspicuous and illustrious.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “For My strength,” it is said, “is made perfect in weakness.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: (2 Cor. xii.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Already these as martyrs have borne testimony: set in the battle against all, they said, “We cannot but speak the things we have seen and heard.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: If the things we speak be false, reprehend them; if true, why hinderest thou?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Such is philosophy!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Those, in perplexity, these in gladness: those covered with exceeding shame, these doing all with boldness: those in fear, these in confidence.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For who, I would ask, were the frightened? those who said, “That it spread no further among people,” or these who said, “we cannot but speak the things we have seen and heard?”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And these had a delight, a freedom of speech, a joy surpassing all; those a despondency, a shame, a fear; for they feared the people.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But these were not afraid of those; on the contrary, while these spake what they would, those did not what they would.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Which were in chains and dangers? was it not these last?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Let us then hold fast to virtue; let not these words end only in delight, and in a certain elevation of the spirits.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: This is not the theatre, for singers (κιθαρώδων), and tragedians, and musicians (κιθαριστὥν), where the fruit consists only in the enjoyment and where the enjoyment itself passes with the passing day.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Nay, would that it were enjoyment alone, and not mischief also with the enjoyment!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But so it is: each man carries home with him much of what he has witnessed there, sticking to him like the infection of a plague: and one indeed, of the younger sort, having culled such snatches of song here and there of those satanic plays, as he could fix in his memory, goes singing them about the house: while another, a senior, and forsooth too staid for such levity, does not this indeed, but what is there spoken, both the preachments and the very words, he remembers it all; and another again, some filthy and absurd ditty.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: From this place you depart, taking nothing with you.—We have laid down a law—nay, not we: God forbid! for it is said, “Call no man your master upon the earth” (Matt. xxiii.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: 8); Christ has laid down a law that none should swear.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Now, say, what has been done with regard to this law?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For I will not cease speaking of it; “lest,” as the Apostle saith, “if I come again, I must not spare.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: (2 Cor. xiii.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: I ask then, have you laid the matter to heart? have you thought of it seriously? have you been in earnest about it, or must we again take up the same subject?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Nay, rather, whether you have or not, we will resume it, that you may think seriously about it, or, if you have laid it to heart, may again do this the more surely, and exhort others also.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: With what then, I pray you, with what shall we begin?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Shall it be with the Old Testament?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For indeed this also is to our shame, that the precepts of the Law, which we ought to surpass, we do not even thus observe!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For we ought not to be hearing such matters as these: these are precepts adapted to the poor Jewish level (τἥς ᾽Ιουδαἳκἥς εὐτελείας): we ought to be hearing those counsels of perfection; “Cast away thy property, stand courageously, and give up thy life in behalf of the Gospel, scorn all the goods of earth, have nothing in common with this present life; if any wrong thee, do him good; if any defraud thee, bless him; if any revile thee, show him honor; be above everything.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Ambros. de Off. i.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: These and such as these are what we ought to be hearing.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But here are we discoursing about swearing; and our case is just the same as if, when a person ought to be a philosopher, one should take him away from the great masters, and set him to spell syllables letter by letter!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Just think now what a disgrace it would be for a man having a flowing beard, and with staff in hand, and cope on shoulders, to go to school with children, and be set the same tasks with them: would it not be above measure ridiculous?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And yet the ridicule which belongs to us is even greater.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For not as the difference between philosophy and the spelling-lesson, so is that between the Jewish polity and ours: no indeed, but as the difference between angels and men.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Say now, if one could fetch down an angel from heaven, and should bid him stand here and listen to our preaching, as one whose duty it is to conform himself thereto, would it not be shameful and preposterous?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But if to be yet, like children, under teaching about these things be ridiculous; what must it be, not even to attend to these things: how great the condemnation, how great the shame!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: To be Christians still, and to have to learn that it is not right to swear!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: However, let us put up with that, lest we incur even worse ridicule.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Well, then, let us speak to you to-day from the Old Testament.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: What does it tell us?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Accustom not thy mouth to swearing; neither use thyself to the naming of the Holy One.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “For as a servant that is continually beaten shall not be without a blue mark, so he that sweareth.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: (Ecclus. xxiii.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: See the discernment of this wise man.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: He did not say, “Accustom not to swearing” thy mind, but “thy mouth”; because being altogether an affair of the mouth, thus it is easily remedied.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For at last it becomes a habit without intention; as for instance, there are many who entering the public baths, as soon as they have passed the threshold, cross (Hom. in 1 Cor. xi.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: 7) themselves (σφραγίζονται).

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: This the hand has got to do, without any one’s bidding, by force of habit.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Again, at the lighting of a candle, often when the mind is intent on something else, the hand makes the sign.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: In the same way also the mouth, without concurrence of the mind, articulates the word, from mere habit, and the whole affair is in the tongue.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Neither use thyself,” he says, “to the naming of the Holy One.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For as a servant that is continually beaten shall not be without a blue mark, so he that sweareth.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: He speaks not here of false oaths, but he cuts down all oaths, and to them also assigns their punishment.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Why then, swearing is a sin.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For such in truth is the soul; full of all these ulcers, all these scars.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But you do not see them?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Yes, this is the mischief of it; and yet you might see if you wished; for God has given you eyes.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: With eyes of this kind did the Prophet see, when he said, “My wounds stink, and are corrupt, because of my foolishness.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: (Ps. xxxviii.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: We have despised God, we have hated that good Name, we have trodden Christ under foot, we have lost all reverence, none names the Name of God with honor.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Yet if you love any one, even at his name you start to your feet; but God you thus continually invoke, and make nothing of it.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Call upon Him for the benefit of your enemy; call upon Him for the salvation of your own soul; then he will be present, then you will delight Him; whereas now you provoke Him to anger.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Call upon Him as Stephen did; “Lord,” he said, “lay not this sin to their charge.” (ch. vii.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Call upon Him as did the wife of Elkanah, with tears and sobs, and prayers.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: I prevent you not, rather I earnestly exhort you to it.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Call upon him as Moses called upon Him, yea, cried, interceding for those who had driven him into banishment.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For you to make mention at random of any person of consideration, is taken as an insult: and do you bandy God about in your talk, in season, out of season?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: I do not want to hinder you from keeping God always in your mind: nay, this I even desire and pray for, only that you should do this, so as to honor Him.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Great good would this have done us, if we had called upon God only when we ought, and for what we ought.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And why, I would ask, were such miracles wrought in the Apostles’ times, and not in ours?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And yet it is the same God, the same Name.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But no, the case is not the same.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For then they called upon Him only for those objects which I have mentioned; whereas we call upon Him not for these, but quite other purposes.—If a man refuse to believe you, and that is why you swear, say to him, “Believe me:” however, if you will needs make oath, swear by yourself.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: I say this, not to set up a law against Christ’s law; God forbid; for it is said.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Let your yea be yea, and your nay, nay (Matt. v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: 37): but by way of coming down to your present level, that I may more easily lead you to the practice of this commandment, and divert you from this tyrannical habit.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: How many who have done well in other respects, have been undone by these practices!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Shall I tell you why it was permitted the ancients to take oaths? (for to take false oaths, was not permitted to them either.)

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Because they swore by idols.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But are you not ashamed to rest in laws, by which they in their infirmity were led on to something better?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: It is true, when I take a Gentile in hand, I do not immediately lay this injunction upon him, but in the first place I exhort him to know Christ; but if the believer, who has both learnt Him and heard Him, must needs crave the same forbearance with the Gentile, what is the use, what the gain (of his Christianity?)—But the habit is strong, and you cannot detach yourself from it?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Well then, since the tyranny of habit is so great, transfer it into another channel.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And how is this to be done? you will ask.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: What I have said often, I say also now; let there be many monitors (λογισταὶ), let there be many examiners and censors (ἐξετασταὶ, δοκιμασταί).

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Say, if you chance to put on your mantle inside out, you allow your servant to correct your mistake, and are ashamed to learn of him, although there is much to be ashamed of in this; and here when you are getting hurt to your soul, are you ashamed to be taught better by another?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: You suffer your menial to put your dress in order, and to fasten your shoes, and will you not endure him that would put your soul in order?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Let even your menial, your child, your wife, your friend, your kinsman, your neighbor, be your teachers on this point.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For as when a wild beast is hunted down from all sides, it is impossible for it to escape; so he that has so many to watch him, so many to reprove him, who is liable to be struck at from all sides, cannot help being on his guard.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The first day he will find it hard to put up with, and the second, and the third; but after that it will come easier, and, the fourth passed, there will not even be anything to do.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Make the experiment, if you doubt me; take it into consideration, I beseech you.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: It is not a trifling matter to be wrong in, nor yet to come right in; on both sides it is great for evil and for good.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: May the good be effected, through the grace and loving-mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ, to Whom with the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, power, and honor, now and ever, world without end.
